results.resolved_title: RTX proteins: a highly diverse family secreted by a common mechanism
results.quote: A large group of homologous cytolytic RTX ‘hemolysins’ is secreted by bacteria of the genus. These include ApxIA, ApxIIA, ApxIIIA of...sp. and AqxA of() or PaxA of()
results.note: Review on RTX/T1SS secretion; abstract states RTX exoproteins export via the type I secretion system. Names PaxA of Pasteurella aerogenes (this entry's organism).

results.resolved_title: RTX proteins: a highly diverse family secreted by a common mechanism
results.note: Entry is Xenorhabdus bovienii paxA (homolog). Review names PaxA only in Pasteurella aerogenes; 0 mentions of Xenorhabdus anywhere in full text. No claim about this organism's protein.

results.resolved_title: Tandem Repeat Proteins and Ank200 are Type 1 Secretion System Substrates Related to the Repeats-in-Toxin Exoprotein Family
results.quote: this work demonstrated thatTRP120, TRP47, TRP32, and Ank200 areT1SS substrates
results.note: Ehrlichia chaffeensis paper; TRP47 = ECH_0166 matches locus_tag. Shown via CRAfT to be T1SS (hemolysin secretion system) substrate, not T4SS.

results.resolved_title: Tandem Repeat Proteins and Ank200 are Type 1 Secretion System Substrates Related to the Repeats-in-Toxin Exoprotein Family
results.quote: this work demonstrated thatTRP120, TRP47, TRP32, and Ank200 areT1SS substrates
results.note: Ehrlichia chaffeensis paper; TRP32 = ECH_0170 matches locus_tag. Shown to be T1SS substrate, not T4SS.

results.resolved_title: Salmonella Typhimurium utilizes a T6SS-mediated antibacterial weapon to establish in the host gut
results.quote: this activity is mediated by the specific interaction of Hcp1 with the antibacterial amidase Tae4
results.note: S. Typhimurium SPI-6 T6SS; Tae4 is the antibacterial amidase effector delivered by the T6SS. Organism and system match.

CrossRef metadata: DOI 10.1038/nsmb.3025 is a single-page editorial/highlight 'A barrier-breaking resolution' by C. Dellisanti, NSMB 22(5):361, May 2015 - not a Tse6/P. aeruginosa T6SS research paper. Cited DOI does not address the Tse6 protein. (crossref.org/works/10.1038/nsmb.3025)
